text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at once, teams work in short cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two weeks. During a sprint, the team focuses on completing tasks from a list called the backlog. These tasks are often described as user stories, which explain a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short meeting called a standup, where members share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any challenges. This iterative development process allows the team to adapt quickly to changes and deliver small, useful improvements regularly.
